The fission fragment beam, separated according to
mass, ionic charge, and kinetic energy by LOHENGRIN,
is stopped in a fast transport tape. The fission
fragments are then transported to a shielded and
absolutely calibrated position at a Ge(Li) detector.
Fractional yields are obtained from absolute counting
of a descendent with a fractional cumulative yield
nearly equal to unity, or from direct counting with
a solid state detector of the total number of
fragments according to mass, kinetic energy and
surface barrier detector for direct counting
of total number of fragments in the beam. No further
Decay gammas of the fission products.
